description:
You are acting as an observability engineer who stores all Grafana dashboard
    JSON definitions under:

      /home/user/observability/dashboards/available/

    and activates (“enables”) a dashboard for live use by creating a symbolic
    link to it inside:

      /home/user/observability/dashboards/enabled/

    Initial state of the system
    ---------------------------
    1. The directory /home/user/observability/dashboards/available/ already
       contains two JSON files:
         • net_traffic.json
         • cpu_load.json
    2. The directory /home/user/observability/dashboards/enabled/ already
       exists but is currently empty.
    3. No file called /home/user/observability/link_actions.log exists yet.

    Goal
    ----
    Enable the dashboard called “net_traffic.json”.

    What you must do
    ----------------
    A. Create a *relative* symbolic link named exactly

         /home/user/observability/dashboards/enabled/net_traffic.json

       that points to the source file

         ../available/net_traffic.json

       (i.e. the link target path must be the literal string
       “../available/net_traffic.json”, not an absolute path).

    B. Immediately after the link is created, record this action by creating
       the log file

         /home/user/observability/link_actions.log

       containing exactly one line (no extra blank lines, no spaces before or
       after the text):

         2024-01-01T00:00:00Z link_created net_traffic.json

       • Make sure the timestamp and wording are *exactly* as above.
       • If the file already exists, append a **new** line with the same text
         (but in the initial state it does not exist).

    Output format that the automated tester will validate
    -----------------------------------------------------
    1. The tester will run `readlink -n /home/user/observability/dashboards/enabled/net_traffic.json`
       and expects the exact output:

       ../available/net_traffic.json

    2. The tester will run `cat /home/user/observability/link_actions.log`
       and expects the exact output (single line, newline-terminated):

       2024-01-01T00:00:00Z link_created net_traffic.json

    In other words, after your work:
       • The symlink exists and resolves correctly.
       • The log file exists and contains the single specified line.

    Hint
    ----
    This can be accomplished with one compound shell command, but any correct
    sequence of commands is acceptable as long as the final state exactly
    matches the two checks described above.
truth:
Pre-existing files and their contents before the agent begins:

    1. /home/user/observability/dashboards/available/net_traffic.json
       --------------------------------------------------------------
       {
           "title": "Network Traffic",
           "panels": [
               { "id": 1, "type": "graph", "title": "Inbound",
                 "targets": [{ "expr": "sum(rate(node_network_receive_bytes_total[5m]))" }]
               },
               { "id": 2, "type": "graph", "title": "Outbound",
                 "targets": [{ "expr": "sum(rate(node_network_transmit_bytes_total[5m]))" }]
               }
           ]
       }

    2. /home/user/observability/dashboards/available/cpu_load.json
       -----------------------------------------------------------
       {
           "title": "CPU Load",
           "panels": [
               { "id": 1, "type": "graph", "title": "Load Average",
                 "targets": [{ "expr": "node_load1" }]
               }
           ]
       }

    3. /home/user/observability/dashboards/enabled/            # Empty directory.

    After successful completion by the agent the test suite will verify:

    • Symlink: /home/user/observability/dashboards/enabled/net_traffic.json
      – Must exist and be a symbolic link.
      – readlink output must be exactly "../available/net_traffic.json".

    • Log file: /home/user/observability/link_actions.log
      – Must exist, regular file, world-readable.
      – File contents must be exactly a single line:

        2024-01-01T00:00:00Z link_created net_traffic.json\n
